seasonality: Hot days should prioritize indoor museums during the afternoon and save viewpoints for morning or evening.
Clear evenings are better for Griffith Observatory views when traffic and parking are manageable.
transport_advice: Los Angeles attractions are spread out, so group Hollywood and museum stops into realistic geographic clusters.
Use rideshare or driving buffers between Hollywood, Miracle Mile, and Westside museums rather than assuming short walking transfers.
planning_tips: Do not combine every famous LA museum and Hollywood landmark into one rushed day.
Pair one film or Hollywood stop with one museum cluster and a viewpoint if timing allows.
Account for traffic when moving between Hollywood, the Getty, and Miracle Mile.
title: Los Angeles Hollywood, Museums, and Culture Planning Notes
content: Seed benchmark knowledge for Los Angeles culture routes: Hollywood landmarks, film-related attractions, museums, and scenic viewpoints are appealing but geographically spread out. A realistic itinerary should cluster stops and treat traffic as a planning constraint.

seasonality: Marine layer can make mornings cooler and cloudier near the coast, so sunset plans need flexibility.
Summer weekends can increase beach-area congestion and parking pressure.
transport_advice: Keep coastal stops on the same side of the city instead of pairing beaches with distant inland museums.
Build in traffic buffers between Santa Monica, Venice, Malibu, and the hotel area.
planning_tips: A beach-focused LA route should choose one coastal corridor instead of jumping between every beach city.
Pair Santa Monica and Venice for a walkable coast day, or choose Malibu for a more driving-heavy scenic day.
Avoid placing a late coastal sunset immediately before a far inland reservation without buffer time.
title: Los Angeles Beaches, Coast, and Outdoor Views Planning Notes
content: Seed benchmark knowledge for Los Angeles coastal routes: beach and ocean-view itineraries work best when the planner selects a coherent coastal corridor such as Santa Monica and Venice or a more scenic Malibu-focused route. The day should account for traffic, parking, and weather near the coast.

seasonality: Hot afternoons should shift longer walks indoors or later in the day.
Evening food routes should account for driving or rideshare return times.
transport_advice: Food neighborhoods in Los Angeles should be grouped by district because cross-city transfers can dominate the itinerary.
Use rideshare, Metro, or driving segments deliberately instead of assuming a continuous pedestrian citywalk.
planning_tips: For food requests, choose one food corridor such as Koreatown, Thai Town, Downtown, or the Arts District and build depth there.
Pair markets and neighborhood walks with nearby cultural stops only when they are geographically close.
Avoid mixing beach, Hollywood, and food-neighborhood routes in the same short day unless the traveler accepts significant travel time.
title: Los Angeles Food, Neighborhoods, and Citywalk Planning Notes
content: Seed benchmark knowledge for Los Angeles food routes: food and neighborhood itineraries should choose a focused district rather than attempt a continuous citywide walk. Koreatown, Thai Town, Downtown, the Arts District, and historic areas can each anchor a coherent half-day.
